acceptanceSignals: Reminder scheduling supports preorder-open and demand-spike scenarios.
Users can opt out and preferences are enforced.
Reminder delivery failures do not impact ordering correctness.

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Rush Reminder Notifications in 0 attempts

reinforcementResults.name: HTTP Contract Sync Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: The new HTTP behavior (`PUT /api/v1/employee/rush-reminder-preferences`) is synchronized with OpenAPI (`contract/openapi/openapi.yaml` and `openapi.json`) and generated client artifacts (`contract/generated/ts-client/*`) in the same change set, and contract tests include route-to-spec coverage plus explicit error-response schema assertions for the new operation.

reinforcementResults.name: High-Risk Write Audit Discipline
reinforcementResults.summary: Rush Reminder Notifications are correctly gated behind a runtime feature flag defaulting to OFF, scheduling is policy-driven (preorder-open and demand-spike) with throttling and preference opt-out enforcement, and reminder delivery failures are handled best-effort without impacting ordering correctness. The feature does not introduce a new high-risk write path that bypasses the existing immutable audit-trail model.

reinforcementResults.name: No Legacy Path Rule
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass. The workspace implements Rush Reminder Notifications behind `PRELAUNCH_RUSH_REMINDER_ENABLED` (default OFF), adds policy-driven scheduling for preorder-open and demand-spike scenarios with throttling and opt-out preference enforcement, and isolates reminder scheduling/delivery as best-effort so delivery failures do not affect ordering correctness. The feature diff shows new contracts/routes/models without retaining superseded legacy/fallback reminder paths.

reinforcementResults.name: Optional Feature Flag Discipline
reinforcementResults.summary: The current feature satisfies the active policy: rush reminders are controlled by a runtime flag (`PRELAUNCH_RUSH_REMINDER_ENABLED`) that defaults to OFF, reminder API exposure is disabled when the flag is OFF (route not mounted and handler returns 404), scheduling is policy-driven with preorder-open and demand-spike scenarios plus throttling and preference opt-out enforcement, and delivery is best-effort/isolated so failures do not affect ordering correctness.

reinforcementResults.name: Data Retention Enforcement
reinforcementResults.summary: Rush Reminder Notifications in this workspace is feature-flagged at runtime with default OFF, supports preorder-open and demand-spike scheduling with policy throttling and opt-out preference enforcement, and keeps reminder delivery best-effort/isolated from ordering paths. The active payroll/order/audit data policy is also technically enforced via retention+purge workflows (including scheduled purge jobs), sensitive payroll field encryption with access-control coverage, and audit-query traceability for purge outcomes.

excludedApproaches: Making reminder delivery a prerequisite for successful ordering.
Sending unthrottled notifications without preference controls.
Hardcoding one static reminder strategy for all contexts.
executionConstraints: Ship behind runtime feature flag default OFF.
Use policy-driven scheduling with throttling and preference checks.
Keep reminder workflows operationally isolated from transaction paths.
name: Rush Reminder Notifications
rationale: Reminder capability is phase-2 and should remain non-critical to MVP flow.
summary: Add optional notification support for open-window and rush cases.
